claim: UN-Water SDG 6.3.1 monitoring estimates that about 56 percent of domestic wastewater flows were safely treated globally in 2022 (no change since 2020), and data gaps mean the safely-treated share could be computed for only a minority of reporting countries.

source.title: SDG indicator 6.3.1: Proportion of domestic and industrial wastewater flow safely treated — UN-Water

method: Reviewed UN-Water SDG6 progress report, wastewater-treatment data, and health-risk analysis.
limitations: The earlier '80 percent discharged untreated' figure was from an older, differently-scoped source and has been replaced with the current UN-Water 6.3.1 domestic-wastewater estimate.
Safely-treated estimates exist for a minority of countries, so the global figure carries substantial coverage uncertainty.
The indicator covers domestic (and where reported industrial) wastewater, not all effluent streams.
It does not provide sub-national treatment grain.
